### **Lead in WA Schools: Making Figures**
This file is used to make plots from a cleaned dataset that includes data from DOH lead water testing, OSPI student enrollment/demographic information, leaded flights, blood lead levels, and lead risk from housing. To run this file, you will need to place the following files in your working directory:

(1) "all_lead_datasets.csv"

### **Step 1. Load R Libraries and Upload Cleaned Data Resulting from "Clean_Lead_Data.Rmd" file**
```{r}
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(sf)
library(scales)

setwd(dir = "~/Dropbox/Katya Dropbox/HCDE pre-tenure (2023-2028)/Research/Lead in WA Schools/")

all_lead_datasets <-read.csv(file = "all_lead_datasets.csv", sep = ",",stringsAsFactors = T)
sd_map <- read_sf("datasets/mapping_geospatial_WA/Geojson Files/Washington_School_District_Boundaries.geojson")

results='hide'
```

### **Step 2. Make State Map of WA-DOH Sampling Program in Districts and Schools (# Samples)
```{r}
#Calculate % of water samples exceeding 5ppb lead in each school district  
state_contamination <- all_lead_datasets %>%
  group_by(school_district, DOH_testing_round) %>%
  summarise(total_samples_district = n(),
            "pct_contamination_district" = 100 * sum(mean_lead_result_ppb > 5, na.rm = TRUE)/total_samples_district)

school_name_contamination <- all_lead_datasets %>%
  group_by(school_name, school_district) %>%
  summarise(total_samples_school = n(),
            "pct_contamination_district" = 100 * sum(mean_lead_result_ppb > 5, na.rm = TRUE)/total_samples_school)

write.csv(school_name_contamination, 
          "datasets/lead_water/2018 Lead Sampling_DOH/correct.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#Add district-level lead contamination data to school district map boundaries
sd_map <- sd_map %>%
  rename(school_district = ShortName,
         esd_num = ESDNum, 
         esd_name = ESDName,
         shape_area = Shape__Area,
         shape_length = Shape__Length,
         sd_geometry = geometry) %>%
select(school_district, 
         esd_num,
         esd_name,
         shape_area,
         shape_length,
         sd_geometry) %>%
 left_join(state_contamination, by = "school_district")
 #Calculate % of water samples exceeding different lead thresholds in each school

school_contamination <- all_lead_datasets %>%
  group_by(school_district, 
           school_name,
           DOH_testing_round,
           x,
           y) %>%
summarise(total_samples_school = n(),
            "lead_1" = 100 * sum(mean_lead_result_ppb > 1, na.rm = TRUE)/total_samples_school,
            "lead_5" = 100 * sum(mean_lead_result_ppb > 5, na.rm = TRUE)/total_samples_school,
            "lead_10" = 100 * sum(mean_lead_result_ppb > 10, na.rm = TRUE)/total_samples_school) 

school_contamination <- school_contamination %>%
  pivot_longer(cols=c(7:9),
            names_to = "contamination_threshold",
               values_to = "pct_contamination_school")  
               

# Convert x,y degree decimal coordinates (lat long) into shapefile and match coordinate systems between datasets
school_contamination_sf <- st_as_sf(school_contamination, coords = c("x", "y"), crs=4326) 
school_contamination_sf <- st_transform(school_contamination_sf, crs = st_crs(sd_map))  

ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = sd_map,
          color = "black",
          size = 0.2,
          aes(fill = pct_contamination_district)) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(na.value = "gray",
                       low = "white",
                       mid = "lightyellow",
                       high = "red",
                       limits = c(0,100))+
  geom_sf(data = school_contamination_sf,
             aes(size = total_samples_school),
          fill = "#3288bd",
          alpha = .2,
          shape = 21,
          stroke = 0.5,
          color = "black")+
  labs(fill = "Percent of Water Samples Exceeding \n5 ppb Lead in School District (%)\n",
       size = "Number of Water Samples \nTested for Lead per School (#)\n") +
   theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),   
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),   
        panel.background = element_blank(), 
        axis.line = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 10,face = "bold"),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 10,face = "bold"))

ggsave(file = "figures/map.png",
       height = 6, width = 10, limitsize = F)
```

### **Step 2. Make Chart of Sample Contamination by Fixture Type
```{r}
#Calculate % Lead Contamination above thresholds by fixture type**

fixture_contamination <- all_lead_datasets %>%
group_by(fixture_type) %>%
summarise(total_samples_fixture_types = n(),
            "lead_1" = 100 * sum(mean_lead_result_ppb > 1, na.rm = TRUE)/total_samples_fixture_types,
            "lead_5" = 100 * sum(mean_lead_result_ppb > 5, na.rm = TRUE)/total_samples_fixture_types,
            "lead_10" = 100 * sum(mean_lead_result_ppb > 10, na.rm = TRUE)/total_samples_fixture_types)

View(fixture_contamination)
fixture_contamination <- fixture_contamination %>%
  pivot_longer(cols=c(3:5),
         names_to = "contamination_threshold",
               values_to = "pct_contamination_fixture") 

ggplot(data = fixture_contamination %>%
           filter(contamination_threshold == "lead_5")%>%
          filter(fixture_type != c( "Other"))) +
  geom_col(aes(y = fct_reorder(fixture_type,pct_contamination_fixture),
           x = pct_contamination_fixture,
           fill = fixture_type),
           width = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "\nPercent Lead Contamination of School Water Fixtures (%)",
       y = "WA School Drinking Water Fixture Types \n") +
   scale_fill_brewer(type = "qual",
                     palette = "Accent")+
   theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12,
                                  face = "bold"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10,
                                 color = "black"),
        legend.position = "none")

ggsave(file = "figures/fixtures.png",
       height = 5, width = 7, limitsize = F)
 
message=FALSE
```


### **Step 4. Plot Lead Trends in School Districts**
```{r}
lead_Seattle <-
ggplot(all_lead_datasets %>% 
         filter(school_district == "Seattle")) +  
  geom_jitter(aes(y = school_name, 
                  x=mean_lead_result_ppb,
                  fill = fixture_type), 
              height = 0.1, width = 0.25,
              shape = 21,
              color = "black",
              size = 4,
              alpha = 0.7,
              stroke = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(labels = c("Other",
                               "Tap/Sink",
                               "Bottle Refill Station",
                               "Water Fountain",
                               "Ice Machine/Fridge",
                               "Pot Filler",
                               "Sprayer/Hose",
                               "Water Cooler"),
                    values = c("black",
                               "orange",
                               "#bae4bc",
                               "#e31a1c",
                               "#0868ac",
                               "blue",
                               "gray",
                               "lightblue"))+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 5, color = "red", size = 1)+
  theme_classic()+
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        legend.title=element_text(size=14, face = "bold"),
        legend.text=element_text(size=12),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size=14, face = "bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size=14, face = "bold"),
        axis.text = element_text(size=12),
        panel.spacing = unit(1,"lines"),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 12))+
  xlab("\nLead Concentration (ppb)")+
  ylab("School Name")+
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(0.25,1,5,10,20,50,100), 
                labels = c("< 1","1","5","10","20","50","100"))+
  guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Fixture Type")) +
  ggtitle("Seattle Public School District") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

ggsave(file = "figures/lead_Seattle.png",
       height = 4, width = 9, limitsize = F)
```
